items.headline: City launching amnesty program to help unlicensed businesses get legal
items.summary: Cupertino is offering a business license amnesty program to shopping centers and other businesses that may be operating without proper licenses. This could affect local compliance and potentially bring in additional city revenue while helping small business owners get legitimate.

items.headline: New incentive programs for retail and startup businesses announced
items.summary: The city is introducing economic development incentives aimed at attracting and supporting retail businesses and startups in Cupertino. This could influence which businesses choose to locate or expand in the area.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing project approved at 490 East Middlefield Road
items.summary: City council approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential project on East Middlefield Road. This represents a new housing development coming to Mountain View.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for Round 4 of California's Prohousing Incentive Program, which provides grants to cities that remove barriers to housing development. This could bring state funding to support local housing initiatives.

items.headline: City seeking public input on how to spend HUD housing grants
items.summary: Santa Clara is holding a public hearing on its draft plan for federal housing funds for 2026-27. This is a chance for residents to weigh in on affordable housing priorities and funding decisions.

items.headline: Safe Parking Program Community Meeting Held
items.summary: Sunnyvale held a community meeting on the Safe Parking program, which provides designated parking for people experiencing homelessness. This is a concrete local response to housing insecurity affecting the South Bay.
